title: megalastrum inaequalifolium var. glabrior
intro: Megalastrum inaequalifolium var. glabrior is a plant species that belongs to the family Ophioglossaceae, commonly known as the adder's tongue ferns. Traditionally, this plant has been used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial activities. The health benefits of Megalastrum inaequalifolium var. glabrior include its ability to aid in wound healing, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory issues such as bronchitis. In terms of herbal preparations, it is often used in teas, infusions, or tinctures to harness its medicinal properties, although more research is needed to fully understand its efficacy and potential interactions.
